content: Unilever's product quality and safety credibility presents a weak overall signal based on available evidence. Disclosure language is vague, offering generic commitments to "safe and high-quality products" without substantive detail, while talent data suggests limited organizational emphasis on this factor with no relevant expertise detected among sampled leadership (n=12, medium confidence). The words-money alignment is neutral given no specific monetary commitments were identified, and with regulatory and supply chain streams excluded, the assessment reflects incomplete coverage rather than comprehensive poor performance.
